Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to examine each case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1 Sequence Receive Check Authorize payment Package Ship Close That matches normative flow inventory sufficient So no violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 Sequence Receive Check Package Authorize payment Ship Close Here package occurs before payment authorization Norm says payment must be authorized before package payment only makes sense once inventory known So violation package before authorize payment Also order of activities is out of order

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Case 3 Sequence Receive Check Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order Here after checking inventory presumably insufficient they authorize payment then cancel then ship

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: According to normative if inventory insufficient after check should go directly to Cancel order then Close order Shipping after cancel is not allowed So violation ship after cancel and after authorize payment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Also maybe authorize payment before cancel is okay But normative says after check insufficient go to Cancel then Close

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So shipping after cancel is definitely wrong

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 4 Sequence Receive Check Cancel order Close order That matches insufficient inventory scenario short sequence No violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 5 Sequence Receive Check Authorize payment Ship order Close order Missing Package items Norm requires package before ship So violation ship before package

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also maybe missing package step is a violation Thus violations Cases 2 3 5 Possibly also case 1 is fine case 4 fine

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check case 2 also missing Authorize payment before package but also package before authorize payment which violates order

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 also violates shipping after cancel
